message:
So last month we had a water main break two blocks over, and suddenly half the neighborhood was dry. I went to grab our emergency water supply and… opened the cabinet to find exactly three dusty bottles from 2019 and a half-empty gallon of apple juice.

My kids thought this was hilarious. My partner less so.

The thing is, I *knew* we needed to top up. I'd thought about it maybe a hundred times. But "preparing" somehow always felt like a rainy-day project, you know? Until the day it actually rained (or didn't, in this case).

We fixed it within hours—totally fine—but it was such a goofy reminder that being *a little* ready beats being *not* ready by a huge margin. And honestly? It doesn't have to be this big, stressful thing. Just a few basics sitting in a corner, quietly waiting.

What's one thing on your "I should really get around to that" prep list that's been sitting there? Curious what everyone's version of my emergency apple juice is 😄
